case_name: Ira Weissman v. Sean Li, M.D.

summary.brief: Ira Weissman v. Sean Li, M.D.. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: In August 2019, plaintiff Ira Weissman underwent a spinal surgical procedure known as minimally invasive lumbar decompression ('MILD').
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Within two days after undergoing the surgery, plaintiff began to experience debilitating pain in both his right leg and foot.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Defendants retained two experts to rebut plaintiff's malpractice claims: a physician who is board-certified in anesthesiology and pain management, and a neurologist.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The defense expert in neurology, meanwhile, opined that the MILD procedure did not cause plaintiff's leg and foot problems, mainly because there was no evidence that the surgery penetrated the thecal sac, which contains the S1 nerve root.
evidence_index.speaker: Defense expert

evidence_index.text: The trial court appropriately granted defendants' motion for summary judgment.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The court did not abuse its discretion in excluding Dr. Gerges's net opinion addressing causation.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: Dr. Gerges testified that nerve injury is a recognized risk of the MILD procedure and emphasized the timing component.
evidence_index.speaker: Plaintiff's expert

evidence_index.text: Plaintiff had further surgery in December 2021 that improved his condition, but continued to need a cane to walk.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Gerges has considerable training and experience in the fields of anesthesia and pain management.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The court did not abuse its discretion in excluding the expert's assertions about causation.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.chief_complaint: Debilitating pain in right leg and foot
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Within two days after undergoing minimally invasive lumbar decompression (MILD) surgery, the patient began to experience debilitating pain in both his right leg and foot.

focused_exam.neurological: Pain in right leg and foot, specific findings not detailed in judgment

critical_findings: Debilitating pain in right leg and foot

requestables.name: Electromyography (EMG)
requestables.description: EMG of the right lower extremity

reveal.result_summary: Chronic Right L5 and acute superimposed on a chronic S1 radiculopathy
reveal.clinical_significance: Indicates nerve irritation and potential injury

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with post-operative pain following spinal surgery

key_events.event: Patient reports debilitating pain in right leg and foot

decision_points.clinical_context: During MILD surgery, decision to operate at L5-S1 level
decision_points.prompt: Should the surgeon operate at the L5-S1 level without explicit consent?

options.description: Operate only on the consented L4-5 level
options.clinical_reasoning: Operating only on consented levels avoids potential legal and ethical issues.

options.description: Operate on both L4-5 and L5-S1 levels
options.clinical_reasoning: Operating on unconsented levels can lead to complications and legal issues.

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant operated on both L4-5 and L5-S1 levels
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant believed it was within the standard of care to modify the method based on intraoperative imaging.

expected_action_court.description: Should have operated only on the consented L4-5 level

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Operating on unconsented levels without clear indication or consent is a deviation from standard care.
explanation.what_should_have_happened: The surgery should have been limited to the consented level unless further consent was obtained.
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony and informed consent requirements.

patient_outcome.description: Patient continued to experience pain and required further surgery in December 2021
patient_outcome.severity: Chronic pain requiring cane for ambulation
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Continued need for a cane to walk

key_findings: Plaintiff's expert testimony on causation was excluded as a net opinion
Summary judgment granted to defendants due to lack of admissible causation evidence

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to provide admissible causation evidence
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Not applicable as no liability was found

factual_timeline.event: Patient experienced debilitating pain in right leg and foot

tests_performed.result: Chronic Right L5 and acute superimposed on a chronic S1 radiculopathy

treatments.outcome: Improved condition but continued need for a cane

complications.complication: Chronic pain in right leg and foot

allegations.allegation: Failure to obtain consent for L5-S1 surgery

court_findings.finding: Plaintiff's expert testimony on causation was excluded as a net opinion
